title: Metals Miner Lumina Ends Trading Day Flat After Toronto IPO
summary: Lumina Metals Corp. shares ended the trading day right where it started after the Canadian company and one of its shareholders raised C$406.2 million ($297 million) in an initial public offering.

title: Novo-Backed Drugmaker Avalyn Soars 64% After Trading Debut
summary: Avalyn Pharma Inc. shares jumped 64% in its trading debut, after the biopharmaceutical company raised $300 million in an upsized US initial public offering.

title: FEMA Reinstates Staff Who Criticized Trump Cuts to Agency
summary: The Federal Emergency Management Agency this week told more than a dozen staffers to return to work after placing them on administrative leave eight months ago for signing an open letter criticizing the Trump administration’s cuts to disaster response, according to people familiar with the decision.

title: Credit Edge: Citi Warns of Private Debt ‘Tourist’ Risk (Podcast)
summary: Rookie private lenders that have to sell in a downturn are a potential threat to credit markets, according to Citi. “If the cycle turns and these tourists, rather than working out loans, just start selling them at below the economic value — what happens to the rest of the market?” Mickey Bhatia, the firm’s head of spread products, tells Bloomberg News’ James Crombie and Bloomberg Intelligence’s Sam Geier in this episode of the Credit Edge podcast. “That’s a big worry,” says Bhatia. Russ Brownback, BlackRock’s deputy CIO of global fixed income and manager of the Strategic Income Opportunities Fund, joins Ira Jersey, Bloomberg Intelligence chief US interest-rate strategist on this Macro Matters edition of the FICC Focus podcast. title: Thomson Family to See $1 Billion Payout as AI Hits Reuters Stock
summary: The Thomson family is on track to reap almost $1 billion from their eponymous company even as its shares have been among the hardest hit by the threat of artificial intelligence.

title: Alberta Energy Minister Believes New Pipeline Will Have Indigenous Support
summary: The Alberta government believes it will have Indigenous support for its plan to build a new oil pipeline to the Pacific coast when it’s unveiled in June.

title: US Critical Mineral Inventory Plan Includes Buying China Metals
summary: The US Export-Import Bank’s proposed stockpiling initiative would initially source critical minerals from anywhere in the world, including China, an official involved in the project has revealed.

title: Citi Retreats From Physical Metals Markets as Bank Cuts Traders
summary: Citigroup Inc. is stepping back from physical trading in industrial metals and has made a number of staff from its commodities team redundant, an employment tribunal in London heard.

title: DOJ Readies Fed Subpoena Appeal After Closing Powell Probe
summary: The US Justice Department plans to file an appeal by Monday against a federal judge’s ruling that blocked prosecutors from using grand jury subpoenas during their investigation into building-cost overruns by the Federal Reserve.

title: Stocks Hit Record at End of Best Month Since 2020: Markets Wrap
summary: Signs of strength in the world’s largest economy fueled investor optimism about the outlook for Corporate America’s earnings, driving stocks to their best month since late 2020.

title: Banamex Sells Global Bonds for First Time Since Citi Sale
summary: Mexico’s Grupo Financiero Banamex has tapped global markets for the first time since Citigroup Inc. started offloading its stake in the bank.

title: Tanger Revenue Beats as Gen Z Reconnects with Physical Stores
summary: Tanger Inc. posted first-quarter earnings that beat expectations and bumped up its full-year guidance, bolstered by robust tenant occupancy rates and a return to physical retail stores by Gen Z shoppers.

title: Gold Rebounds as Speculation of Yen Intervention Weakens Dollar
summary: Gold bounced back from a three-day decline as the dollar sank and traders weighed the prospect of a fresh US military engagement in Iran.

title: Meta Sells $25 Billion of Debt as Investor Worry, Fatigue Builds
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. sold $25 billion of investment-grade bonds, hitting the market with a jumbo deal for the second time in six months as investors are starting to show some signs of fatigue.

title: Alphabet Soars, S&P Hits Another Record | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gresham House Said to Plan €1 Billion Fund for Battery Storage
summary: Gresham House plans to raise a fund targeting roughly €1 billion ($1.2 billion) to give investors access to the growing market for battery-storage systems, according to a person familiar with the matter.

title: Wayfair CFO Sees Growth Despite 'Challenged' Consumer
summary: Wayfair CFO Kate Gulliver said despite an earnings miss by the furniture seller the company feels 'great' about the growth outlook. Gulliver said Wayfiar is less focused on when 'challenged' consumers will recover, but the strength of internal initiatives that she believes will drive growth.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Dollar Wraps Worst Month Since June as Haven Trade Unwinds
summary: The US dollar wrapped up its worst month since June as the prospect of peace talks to end the Iran war led traders to unravel bets on the haven currency.

title: Inside Cadillac's New Formula 1 Team
summary: Dan Towriss, CEO, TWG Motorsports discusses launching Cadillac’s Formula 1 team and the role of institutional capital in global motorsport with Bloomberg's Hannah Elliott at Bloomberg House Miami 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AIG Profit Jumps as CEO Zaffino’s Strategic Deals Take Effect
summary: American International Group Inc. reported first-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations, driven in part by a jump in net premiums written and lower catastrophe losses.

title: S&P 500 Jumps 1% to Record High, Posting Best Month Since 2020
summary: US stocks rallied to new heights on Thursday as investors focused on resilient US economic growth and corporate earnings, while oil prices fell from a four-year high.

title: Pershing Square Shares Rebound After Ackman Bought Stock
summary: Shares in Bill Ackman’s new $5 billion listed investment fund and his asset management company rebounded in the second day after their IPO, as the hedge fund billionaire revealed he had put more of his own money behind the deal.

title: US Treasuries Draw Buyers After Selloff as Oil Surge Stalls
summary: Treasuries rose as the recent surge in oil prices stalled and bond investors were drawn to 30-year yields that topped 5% for the first time this year.

title: 'No Signs Whatsoever' of High-End Consumer Pullback: Hyatt CEO
summary: Mark Hoplamazian, president & CEO at Hyatt Hotels Corporation, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Markets." Hyatt shares climb after the hotel operator reported adjusted earnings-per-share for the first quarter that topped analysts’ expectations. Analysts were encouraged by the update, which showed that the company’s luxury chains continue to outperform. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Citi’s Bhatia Warns of Private Credit ‘Tourists’ Forced to Sell
summary: Private credit rookies selling into a downturn poses a potential threat to corporate debt, Citigroup Inc.’s Mickey Bhatia warned.

title: Ares, JPMorgan Ink $800 Million Private Credit Deal for GoodLife
summary: A group including Ares Management Corp. provided around $800 million in a private credit deal to finance Apollo Global Management Inc.’s investment in the GoodLife Group, which operates health clubs in Canada, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: How Ketamine Spawned a $1.7 Billion J&J Blockbuster Drug
summary:
Spravato, the ketamine-derived nasal spray from Johnson & Johnson, was once seen as a long shot. It was difficult to deliver, heavily regulated and slow to catch on. Find out how it became a billion-dollar drug and part of a new model for treating depression.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: New York Life Raises C$1.1 Billion in Maple Bond Sale
summary: New York Life Global Funding sold C$1.1 billion ($810 million) of Canadian-dollar debt maturing in seven years, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Private Credit Giants Try to Reassure Investors on AI Risks to Software Bets
summary: Three of the biggest names in private credit moved to reassure investors this week about the AI risks facing their software borrowers, deploying proprietary “score cards” and outside consultants.

title: Bombardier CEO Says Debt ‘Melting Away’; Shares Soar to 23-Year High
summary: Canadian jet manufacturer Bombardier Inc. surged after it raised its cash flow outlook for this year and reported strong interest in its defense products.

title: Oil Retraces From Four-Year High as War Fears Dampen Liquidity
summary: Brent crude cooled off after ripping to a four-year high as the market braced for a protracted US war with Iran, in a session marked by machine-led trading and thin volumes.

title: Pemex Posts Third Straight Loss After $16.5 Billion Debt Payment
summary: Petroleos Mexicanos posted its third straight quarterly loss as large debt payments ate into earnings of Mexico’s state-owned oil giant.

title: Blue Owl Scrutiny to Ramp Up as Shares Near ‘Doomsday’ Level
summary: Blue Owl Capital Inc., whose battered stock has served as a proxy for concerns over the health of the $1.8 trillion private credit industry, will face fresh scrutiny from investors and Wall Street analysts with the release of its first-quarter earnings.  Bloomberg's David Havens joins to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: NY Pied-à-Terre Tax Needs Significant Rates to Earn $500 Million
summary: Luxury second homes in New York will have to be taxed at a “significant” amount to generate the $500 million of revenue the city is counting on to help close a gaping budget hole, according to a new study from New York City Comptroller Mark Levine’s office.

title: Iran’s New Leader Pledges to Guard Nuclear and Missile Tech
summary: Iran’s new supreme leader gave a rare statement on Thursday, vowing not to give up the country’s nuclear or missile technologies and signaling Tehran would keep control of the Strait of Hormuz. Natasha Hall, Associate Fellow at Chatham House joins to discuss on Balance of Power. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Senate Bans Members, Staff From Trading on Prediction Markets
summary: The US Senate voted unanimously Thursday to ban its members and staff from trading on fast-growing prediction markets, highlighting concerns about insider trading on the platforms after a US soldier was accused of using classified information to profit from trades.

title: Yen Soars as Japan Intervenes After Issuing ‘Final’ Warning
summary: The yen surged as much as 3%, its biggest intraday gain in almost two years, after Japan intervened in the foreign-exchange market, hours after officials delivered a “final” warning to investors against selling the currency.
